title: 'overlay' filesystem support being missing is permanently cached (until reboot?)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

(This follows from #1516)

I have a system which tries to start `podman` on boot, which I've just updated to a new kernel.  Something seems to have changed, but whilst the `overlay` module was previous being auto-loaded is no longer is... but this I can fix.

The issue is that if `podman` is *ever* started without overlay support being present, then it will cache this status and continue to report that overlay support is unavailable even when it is.  And if `podman` is started on boot, this means that this is maintained for the entire session or until the magic file is removed.

Admittedly, if the `overlay` module is manually loaded on boot then this would be resolved in the next session (so long as the status file is removed on shutdown?) but this seems an unhelpful and non-obvious way to do things: if overlay support has _become_ available then why not clear the error status?

(I've been using `podman` for some time now and this almost had me downgrading the kernel to figure out what could have changed!  I'm still not entirely sure how `overlay` support was present/autoloaded before and isn't now, although it could have been moved to a module from being built-in in the upstream kernel update...?)

```
$ sudo podman info ; echo ; sudo ls -l /storage/containers/podman/run/overlay/overlay-false ; echo ; sudo cat /storage/containers/podman/run/overlay/overlay-false ; echo ; sudo rm /storage/containers/podman/run/overlay/overlay-false ; sudo podman version
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/storage/containers/podman/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

-rw-r--r-- 1 root sys 179 Nov  7 16:27 /storage/containers/podman/run/overlay/overlay-false

kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/storage/containers/podman/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Git Commit:   a6493ae6904a5b7dde2395a374cb77fc45c8a3bc
Built:        Sun Oct 24 03:43:26 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
@giuseppe @rhvgoyal PTAL
Shouldn't the overlay driver be autoloaded?

this is a configuration issue, since `runRoot` is supposed to be on a volatile file system like tmpfs that doesn't survive a reboot.

Please change the `runRoot` to be on tmpfs.

We do not have a runtime check since there are use cases when this is fine, e.g., running in a nested container

I'm half-necro-bumping this issue because it was the first hit when I searched for the string

    podman 'overlay' is not supported over extfs

Just wanted to add that this may also happen if your kernel was not configured with `CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS`.
There is a workaround mentioned on [SO](https://unix.stackexchange.com/a/689182), which is using the `fuse-overlayfs` package. You then need to modify the `mount_program` option, eg in `/etc/containers/storage.conf`.

Hope I'm not polluting the bug tracker. I tried to keep it short anyway.
